events.text: Patient is a 49-year-old female of Asian (Chinese) ethnicity, non-Hispanic, BMI 22.4, with no significant past medical history.
At age 48, patient presented with bright red blood per rectum and tenesmus; flexible sigmoidoscopy revealed a rectosigmoid mass (C19.9).

Biopsy of rectosigmoid mass showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with no mucinous or signet ring features; tumor invading through muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissue.

Next-generation sequencing identified KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA H1047R co-mutation, HER2 non-amplified, APC frameshift mutation; microsatellite stable (MSS) with proficient mismatch repair by immunohistochemistry.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 48 showed a rectosigmoid junction mass with perirectal lymphadenopathy and a solitary 2.8 cm metastasis in hepatic segment VI; no pulmonary metastases; staged T3N1bM1a.

At age 48, baseline CEA was 56 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 42 U/mL; tumor board discussion noted young age, good performance status, left-sided primary with solitary liver metastasis potentially amenable to conversion therapy.
At age 48, patient initiated first-line FOLFOX (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2, 5-FU, leucovorin) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks with goal of liver metastasis downsizing for resection.
After cycle 3 of FOLFOX, patient developed grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with tingling in fingertips, monitored without dose change.

Restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFOX-bevacizumab showed excellent partial response with rectosigmoid mass decreased in size and hepatic metastasis reduced from 2.8 cm to 1.2 cm; CEA decreased to 8.3 ng/mL.
At age 49, tumor board recommended proceeding with hepatic metastasectomy followed by resection of primary given excellent response.
At age 49, patient underwent laparoscopic wedge resection of segment VI hepatic metastasis with intraoperative ultrasound confirmation of no additional lesions.
Hepatic wedge resection showed residual moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with 60% treatment effect, negative margins (closest margin 0.8 cm).
At age 49, patient underwent low anterior resection of rectosigmoid tumor with total mesorectal excision and diverting loop ileostomy.

Low anterior resection specimen demonstrated residual moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, ypT2N1a (1 of 16 lymph nodes positive), negative circumferential resection margin, and moderate treatment response.
At age 49, postoperative short-course radiation therapy (25 Gy in 5 fractions) was delivered to the pelvis given close circumferential margin concerns, though final margin was negative.
At age 49, postoperative FOLFOX (without bevacizumab) resumed for 4 additional cycles to complete perioperative chemotherapy.
At age 49, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy after cumulative 10 cycles of oxaliplatin, with numbness in hands and feet affecting fine motor tasks.
Surveillance CT at age 49 (3 months post-completion of therapy) showed no evidence of recurrent disease; CEA was 3.1 ng/mL; CA 19-9 was 15 U/mL.
At age 49, loop ileostomy was reversed after confirmation of anastomotic integrity by contrast enema.
At age 49, patient entered surveillance with CT scans every 3 months, CEA every 3 months, and colonoscopy planned at 1 year; ECOG 0, residual grade 1 neuropathy.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
